description: Workflow automation I'm building applies a three-way merge via `patch` but it's silently producing garbage on one specific file. The setup: `/home/user/workflow/base.txt` is the common ancestor, `/home/user/workflow/theirs.txt` is the upstream version, and `/home/user/workflow/ours.txt` is our local version. I generate a diff from base→theirs with `diff -u base.txt theirs.txt > upstream.patch`, then apply it to ours with `patch ours.txt upstream.patch`. Works fine on 99% of files but this one ends up mangled — hunks land in wrong places, context lines don't match, patch complains about offsets but applies anyway with `--force`. The weird part is both theirs and ours contain all the same sections as base, just with different edits in different places. I've eyeballed the files and nothing jumps out. Need `/home/user/workflow/merged.txt` that correctly incorporates changes from both theirs and ours relative to base — a proper three-way merge, no lost edits, no duplicated content.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/workflow/ contains base.txt, theirs.txt, ours.txt
- base.txt (the common ancestor) contains 80 lines of configuration-like content organized into 5 sections: [database], [cache], [logging], [auth], [features]. Each section has ~15 lines of key=value pairs.
- theirs.txt modifies the [cache] section (changes 3 values) and adds 2 new keys to [features].
- ours.txt modifies the [logging] section (changes 2 values) and deletes 1 key from [auth].
- THE TRAP: base.txt has mixed line endings — lines 1-40 use LF, lines 41-80 use CRLF. This happened because someone edited the second half on Windows and committed without normalization.
- theirs.txt was produced by a tool that normalized everything to LF.
- ours.txt preserved the original mixed line endings from base.txt.
- When `diff -u base.txt theirs.txt` runs, the CRLF/LF mismatch in the second half causes context lines to not match byte-for-byte. The diff captures theirs' changes correctly but the context includes the CR characters from base where they exist.
- When `patch ours.txt upstream.patch` runs, the context lines in hunks affecting the second half (where theirs normalized but ours kept CRLF) fail to match. With --force, patch applies at wrong offsets or skips hunks, corrupting the merge.
- The symptom (hunks at wrong offsets, failed context match) looks like a line-number problem but the root cause is invisible CR bytes causing context mismatch.
- diff, patch, diff3, and merge utilities are available. GNU coreutils including dos2unix is available. Python 3 with standard library is available.
- /home/user/workflow/ is writable.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/workflow/merged.txt exists and contains:
  - The [database] section unchanged from base
  - The [cache] section with theirs' 3 value changes
  - The [logging] section with ours' 2 value changes  
  - The [auth] section with ours' deleted key
  - The [features] section with theirs' 2 added keys
- All semantic edits from both branches are present, none lost, none duplicated.
- merged.txt has consistent line endings (either all LF or all CRLF, agent's choice).
- Running `diff3 -m ours_normalized theirs_normalized base_normalized` (with all files normalized to same line endings) would produce content semantically identical to merged.txt — no conflict markers remain.

Invariants:
- base.txt, theirs.txt, ours.txt unchanged (byte-identical to initial state).
- No section content invented or fabricated — all values must trace to one of the three input files.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- merged.txt must contain theirs' [cache] changes AND ours' [logging] changes AND ours' [auth] deletion AND theirs' [features] additions. Grader will check for presence of specific key=value pairs unique to each branch's edits:
  - From theirs: cache_ttl=3600, cache_backend=redis, cache_prefix=v2_ (in [cache]) and feature_dark_mode=true, feature_export=csv,json (in [features])
  - From ours: log_level=debug, log_rotate=weekly (in [logging]) and auth_timeout key must NOT be present (was deleted)
- `grep -c $'\r' /home/user/workflow/merged.txt` must return 0 OR `file merged.txt` shows consistent line endings — no mixed endings in output.
- merged.txt must have exactly 82 lines (base 80 + 2 new feature keys from theirs - 0 net from ours' logging changes which are value changes not additions - 1 deleted auth key = 81... wait, let me recalc: base has 80, theirs adds 2 to features = 82 in theirs, ours deletes 1 from auth = 79 in ours. Merged should have: 80 + 2 (theirs features) - 1 (ours auth deletion) = 81 lines). So exactly 81 lines.
name: task_000000_f34641ea
